draft.introduction: Surah Al-A’raf (The Heights) narrates the story of Adam and Iblis (Satan), the sending of prophets to various nations, and the fate of those who rejected them. It is one of the Quran’s longest narrative surahs.

translation.text: And We created you, then fashioned you, then said to the angels: ‘Prostrate before Adam.’ They prostrated, except Iblis—he was not of those who prostrated. God said: ‘What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?’ He said: ‘I am better than he. You created me from fire and created him from clay.’

translator_notes.note: Iblis’s refusal to prostrate before Adam on grounds of material superiority (fire over clay) introduces the Quranic theme that pride (kibr) is the root sin. In Sufi tradition, some mystics debated whether Iblis’s refusal was pure disobedience or a twisted form of devotion to God alone.
